title: What is a Function?
description: Write reusable blocks of code to stay organized.

slides.title: Functions 🛠️
slides.content:
A function is a block of code which only runs when it is called.

You can pass data into a function. Functions are used to perform certain actions, and they are important for reusing code.

slides.title: Why Functions? ♻️
slides.content:
Why write the exact same 10 lines of code five different times in your program when you could write it *once* inside a function, and then just ask the function to run five times?

Code reuse saves you tons of time and effort!

quizData.question: What is the primary benefit of functions?

options.text: They make the program run slower
To crash the code on purpose

quizData.explanation: Functions allow us to define code once and use it many times.

slides.title: Pre-defined Methods 🧠
slides.content:
You already know some built-in functions, like `main()`, which is used to start the program.

You can also create your own custom functions!

quizData.question: A function runs its code automatically the moment the program starts.
quizData.explanation: No! A function only runs WHEN it is explicitly 'called' by the programmer (except for main(), which the OS calls automatically).
